qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in URLLoader and DocumentParser allows access to internal network resources
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a WAF bypass in TableChatAgent's pandas_eval via _literal_ok() returning False and unrestricted dunder attribute access in langroid/utils/pandas_utils.py. This finding concerns URLLoader/DocumentParser URL fetching without validation in langroid/parsing/url_loader.py and langroid/parsing/document_parser.py. Different vulnerable component, different root cause, different vulnerability class (SSRF vs code injection).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying_vuln finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a WAF bypass in langroid/utils/pandas_utils.py where _literal_ok() returns False instead of raising UnsafeCommandError on invalid input, combined with unrestricted access to dangerous dunder attributes (__init__, __globals__, __builtins__), enabling RCE via TableChatAgent's pandas_eval tool. The sole qualifying finding is an SSRF in URLLoader/DocumentParser (different component, different root cause, different vulnerability class). The eval-table-chat-agent finding, which is thematically closest, is marked non_qualifying_vuln and missed the actual WAF bypass mechanism, focusing instead on the full_eval=True configuration path.
evidence: Advisory root cause: _literal_ok() in langroid/utils/pandas_utils.py returns False instead of raising UnsafeCommandError, plus unrestricted dunder attribute access
ssrf-url-loader references langroid/parsing/url_loader.py and langroid/parsing/document_parser.py — different component from TableChatAgent/pandas_utils.py
eval-table-chat-agent (non_qualifying_vuln) identifies TableChatAgent pandas_eval but misses the WAF bypass, attributing risk only to full_eval=True configuration
